prose:
Method provenance and findings:

• Property: condensed Fukui function f(+) (electrophilic attack) value at the most electrophilic atom of acrolein.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry: single-point on the user-supplied acrolein.xyz (no re-optimization).
• Environment: gas phase.
• Charge/multiplicity: neutral singlet for N state; doublet cation (+1) and doublet anion (-1) for N±1.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill; SCF converged for all three charge states.
• Key numerical headline: f(+) on atom index 3 (β-carbon) = 0.64232102 e⁻, identifying it as the most electrophilic center.

Warnings from engine (verbatim):
• fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• schema_header: common header present
